Find the values of \(x\) which satisfy both \(x+3\gt0\) and \(-2x\lt1\).
A. \(x\gt-3\)
B. \(x\gt-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
C. \(x\gt\dfrac{1}{2}\)
D. \(-3\lt x\lt-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
E. \(-3\lt x\lt\dfrac{1}{2}\)

A. \(6\)
B. \(8\)
C. \(9\)
D. \(12\)
E. \(18\)

A

A. \(9\text{ cm}^2\)

A man bought two books at \(\$30\) and \(\$70\) respectively. He sold the first one at a profit of \(20\%\) and the second one at a loss of \(10\%\). On the whole, he
A. lost \(1\%\).
B. lost \(10\%\).
C. gained \(1\%\).
D. gained \(10\%\).
E. gained \(13\%\).

The \(1^\text{st}\) and \(10^\text{th}\) terms of an arithmetic sequence are \(2\) and \(29\) respectively. The \(20^\text{th}\) term of the sequence is
A. \(56\).
B. \(58\).
C. \(59\).
D. \(60\).
E. \(62\).

Which of the following could be a geometric sequence / geometric sequences?
I. \(3,3,3,3,3,\ldots\)
II. \(9,99,999,9\,999,\ldots\)
III. \(10,-100,1\,000,-10\,000,\ldots\)
A. III only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

Consider the three straight lines
\(L_1:6x+4y-3=0\),
\(L_2:y=\dfrac{3}{2}x+4\) and
\(L_3:6x-4y+3=0\).
Which of the following is / are true?
I. \(L_1 \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} L_2\)
II. \(L_2 \perp L_3\)
III. \(L_1 \perp L_3\)
A. I only
B. III only
C. II only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

Two fair dice are thrown. Find the probability that at least one \(6\) occurs.
A. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{6}\)
C. \(\dfrac{5}{18}\)
D. \(\dfrac{7}{36}\)
E. \(\dfrac{11}{36}\)

A bag contains six balls which are marked with the numbers \(-3\), \(-2\), \(1\), \(1\), \(2\) and \(3\) respectively. Two balls are drawn randomly from the bag. Find the probability that the sum of the numbers drawn is zero.
A. \(\dfrac{1}{30}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{10}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{5}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{2}\)

\(\{x,x+2,x+4,x+6,x+8\}\) and \(\{x+1,x+3,x+5,x+7,x+9\}\) are two groups of numbers. Which of the following is / are true?
I. The two groups of numbers have the same range.
II. The two groups of numbers have the same standard deviation.
III. The two groups of numbers have the same mean.
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. I and III only

III. \(\Delta EAC\) is an isosceles triangle

B. III only
C. I and II only

In the figure, a solid wooden sphere of radius \(r\) cm is to be cut into a cube of side \(3\) cm. Find the smallest possible value of \(r\).

B. \(2\)
C. \(3\sqrt{2}\)
D. \(3\sqrt{3}\)
E. \(3\sqrt{2}\)

\(y\) varies directly as \(x^2\) and inversely as \(\sqrt{z}\). If \(y=1\) when \(x=2\) and \(z=9\), find \(y\) when \(x=1\) and \(z=4\).
A. \(\dfrac{2}{3}\)
B. \(\dfrac{3}{8}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{16}\)
D. \(\dfrac{3}{32}\)
E. \(\dfrac{9}{26}\)

Tea \(A\) and tea \(B\) are mixed in the ratio \(x:y\) by weight. \(A\) costs \(\$80/\text{kg}\) and \(B\) costs \(\$100/\text{kg}\). If the cost of \(A\) is increased by \(10\%\) and that of \(B\) is decreased by \(12\%\), the cost of the mixture per kg remains unchanged. Find \(x:y\).
A. \(1:1\)
B. \(2:3\)
C. \(3:2\)
D. \(5:6\)
E. \(6:5\)
